patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/471

Add a brief installed package/extension health summary to `pm context` so agents can diagnose extension-related issues without separate `pm package doctor` runs. Must respect the context token budget (see GH-445 tag-folding work pm-ishm) — summary line(s), not full diagnostics; opt-out via flag/--fields.
GH-471: pm context includes an installed package/extension health summary

Dogfood GH-471: pm context shows item counts but nothing about extension/package health, forcing separate doctor/health invocations to understand project state. Duplicate check: searched context extension health summary — pm-ishm (GH-445) covers context tag noise only, no overlap.
pm context output includes a brief installed-packages line with health status (ok/degraded per package); stays token-lean and can be suppressed via a flag or --fields; covered by specs

author: claude-code-agent
message: Release claim after documenting current bundle progress; not closing unresolved scope.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Context health summary consumes the same health checks that currently hang during vectorization

author: maintainer-agent
message: Graph pass #92: context package-health summary must consume the same renderer-aware doctor semantics nf7q fixes, or it inherits the false-positive collision warnings
